# SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector - Full Documentation

# SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector 2.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector (SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector) provides full support for the SOAP APIs exposed by
SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, and starting with version 2.1, also provides support for SAP S/4HANA
on-premise ERP. This connector enables you to automatically discover SOAP services enabled on your
SAP S/4HANA instance and to invoke the SOAP service and associated operations.

For software requirements and compatibility information, see the
[SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/sap-s4-hana-soap-cloud-connector-release-notes-mule-4).

## Before You Begin

Before you create an app and use this connector, you must:

- Have a developer account or other valid SAP S/4HANA instance.
- Have a MuleSoft Enterprise license.
- Enable discovery service in your SAP S/4HANA instance.
- Have a communication user that is assigned to the corresponding communication arrangements for the
  desired APIs.
- Be familiar with:
  - SAP S/4HANA SOAP
  - Mule runtime engine (Mule)
  - Anypoint Connectors
  - Anypoint Studio essentials

## Common Use Cases For the Connector

Send an XML request to SAP S/4HANAâs SOAP API and print a response.

## Using Exchange Templates and Examples

[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) provides templates you can use as a starting
point for your app, as well as examples that illustrate a complete solution.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites and experiment with templates and examples, you are ready to
create an app with [Anypoint Studio](sap-s4hana-soap-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/sap-s4-hana-soap-cloud-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
- [SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector on Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-sap-s4hana-soap-cloud-connector/)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector 2.6 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Send an XML Request and Print the Response

This example sends an XML request to SAP S/4HANAâs SOAP API and prints the response:

![First flow with Listener and Parse Template and second flow with Listener, Transform Message, Logger, Invoke, Logger, and Transform Message](_images/sap-s4hana-soap-demo-flow.png)

### POM File for This Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/maven-v4_0_0.xsd">
	<modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

	<groupId>com.mycompany</groupId>
	<artifactId>sap-hana-soap-demo</artifactId>
    	<version>2.1.0</version>
    	<packaging>mule-application</packaging>

	<name>sap-hana-soap-demo</name>

    <properties>
		<project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
		<project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>

		<app.runtime>4.2.0</app.runtime>
		<mule.maven.plugin.version>3.2.7</mule.maven.plugin.version>
	</properties>

	<build>
	   <plugins>
	      <plugin>
		 <groupId>org.mule.tools.maven</groupId>
		 <artifactId>mule-maven-plugin</artifactId>
		 <version>${mule.maven.plugin.version}</version>
		 <extensions>true</extensions>
	      </plugin>
	   </plugins>
	</build>

	<dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-http-connector</artifactId>
            <version>1.5.3</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-sockets-connector</artifactId>
            <version>1.1.5</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
    	<dependency>
            <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-sap-s4hana-soap-cloud-connector</artifactId>
            <version>2.1.0</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
	</dependencies>

	<repositories>
          <repository>
            <id>anypoint-exchange</id>
            <name>Anypoint Exchange</name>
            <url>https://maven.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v1/maven</url>
            <layout>default</layout>
        </repository>
        <repository>
            <id>mulesoft-releases</id>
            <name>MuleSoft Releases Repository</name>
            <url>https://repository.mulesoft.org/releases/</url>
            <layout>default</layout>
        </repository>
    </repositories>
    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>mulesoft-releases</id>
            <name>mulesoft release repository</name>
            <layout>default</layout>
            <url>https://repository.mulesoft.org/releases/</url>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

</project>
```

### XML Code for This Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:s4hana-soap="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap/current/mule-s4hana-soap.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<configuration-properties
		doc:name="Configuration properties"
		file="mule-artifact.properties" />

	<s4hana-soap:config
		name="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Config"
		doc:name="SAP S4 HANA SOAP Config" >
		<s4hana-soap:basic-authentication-connection
			baseUrl="${config.serviceBaseUrl}"
			username="${config.username}"
			password="${config.password}" />
	</s4hana-soap:config>
	<flow name="htmlPageFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/"/>
		<parse-template doc:name="Parse Template"  location="form.html"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="queryFlow"  >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"  config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/query"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message"  >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
ns ns0 http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal/Global
---
{
	ns0#CreditManagementAccountByIDQuery: {
		MessageHeader: {
			ID: "@" as String,
			CreationDateTime: now()
		},
		Selection: {
			DebtorPartyInternalID: attributes.queryParams['DebtorPartyInternalID'] as String,
			CreditsegmentInternalID: attributes.queryParams['CreditsegmentInternalID'] as String
		},
		LanguageCode: "ZZZ" as String
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO"
			doc:name="Logger"
			message="Request #[payload]"/>
		<s4hana-soap:invoke
			doc:name="Invoke"
			config-ref="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Config"
			service="https://mysap.s4hana.instance.com/sap/bc/srt/wsdl_ext/flv_10002S111AD1/srvc_url/sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/creditmanagementaccountbyidqu1"
			operation="CreditManagementAccountByIDQuery_In">
		</s4hana-soap:invoke>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"  message="Response #[payload]"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Send an XML Request and Receive the Replication Outbound Message

In this example, an XML request is sent to SAP S/4HANAâs SOAP API and then receives the replication
outbound message, which is saved to the object store:

![First flow with Listener and Parse Template and second flow with Outbound Message Listener, Logger, Transform Message, Store, and Logger](_images/sap-s4hana-soap-soource-demo-flow1.png)

This flow is used to get the outbound message from the object store:

![First flow with Listener, Retrieve, Transform Message, and Logger and second flow with Listener, Transform Message, Invoke, Logger, and Transform Message](_images/sap-s4hana-soap-soource-demo-flow2.png)

### POM File for This Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/maven-v4_0_0.xsd">
	<modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors.examples</groupId>
    <artifactId>sap-hana-soap-demo</artifactId>
    <version>2.1.0</version>
    <packaging>mule-application</packaging>

	<name>s4-hana-source-demo</name>

    <properties>
		<project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
		<project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>

		<app.runtime>4.3.0</app.runtime>
		<mule.maven.plugin.version>3.3.5</mule.maven.plugin.version>
	</properties>

	<build>
		<plugins>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
				<artifactId>maven-clean-plugin</artifactId>
				<version>3.0.0</version>
			</plugin>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.mule.tools.maven</groupId>
				<artifactId>mule-maven-plugin</artifactId>
				<version>${mule.maven.plugin.version}</version>
				<extensions>true</extensions>
				<configuration>
				<classifier>mule-application</classifier>
                </configuration>
			</plugin>
		</plugins>
	</build>

	<dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-http-connector</artifactId>
            <version>1.5.17</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-sap-s4hana-soap-cloud-connector</artifactId>
            <version>2.1.0</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
		<dependency>
            <groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
            <artifactId>mule-objectstore-connector</artifactId>
            <version>1.1.5</version>
            <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

	<repositories>
          <repository>
            <id>anypoint-exchange-v2</id>
            <name>Anypoint Exchange</name>
            <url>https://maven.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/v2/maven</url>
            <layout>default</layout>
        </repository>
        <repository>
            <id>mulesoft-releases</id>
            <name>MuleSoft Releases Repository</name>
            <url>https://repository.mulesoft.org/releases/</url>
            <layout>default</layout>
        </repository>
    </repositories>
    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>mulesoft-releases</id>
            <name>mulesoft release repository</name>
            <layout>default</layout>
            <url>https://repository.mulesoft.org/releases/</url>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

</project>
```

### XML Code for This Example

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:wsc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/wsc"
	xmlns:s4hana="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana" xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:s4hana-soap="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana-soap/current/mule-s4hana-soap.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/s4hana/current/mule-s4hana.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTPS_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8082" protocol="HTTPS"/>
	</http:listener-config>

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<s4hana-soap:listener-config name="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Listener_config" doc:name="SAP S4 HANA SOAP Listener config" httpListenerConfig="HTTPS_Listener_config" />

	<s4hana-soap:config name="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Config" doc:name="SAP S4 HANA SOAP Config" >
		<s4hana-soap:basic-authentication-connection baseUrl="${config.serviceBaseUrl}" username="${config.username}" password="${config.password}" />
	</s4hana-soap:config>

	<os:object-store name="Product" doc:name="Object store" entryTtl="30" />

	<flow name="html-page-flow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/"/>
		<parse-template doc:name="Parse Template" location="form.html"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="product-message-type-flow">
		<s4hana-soap:outbound-message-listener doc:name="Outbound Message Listener" config-ref="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Listener_config" messageType="ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequestMessage" path="/productmdmbulkreplicaterequest" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message='#["Outbound message received in the Product flow"]'/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<os:store doc:name="Store" key="#[payload.ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequestMessage.ProductMDMReplicateRequestMessage.Product.ProductInternalID]" objectStore="Product"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="#[payload]" />
	</flow>

	<flow name="get-outbound-message-flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/get"/>
		<os:retrieve doc:name="Retrieve" key="#[attributes.queryParams.productId]" objectStore="Product">
			<os:default-value ><![CDATA[There is no such productId]]></os:default-value>
		</os:retrieve>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>

	<flow name="create-product-flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/create"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/xml
ns ns0 http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global
---
{
	ns0#ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequestMessage: {
		MessageHeader: {
			ID: "FA163E6728911EDAA5BFCEE21420A568",
			UUID: "fa163e67-2891-1eda-a5bf-cee21420a568",
			CreationDateTime: "20200528121755",
			SenderBusinessSystemID: "SOBASIC",
			RecipientBusinessSystemID: "0M2JP31"
		},
		ProductMDMReplicateRequestMessage: {
			MessageHeader: {
				ID: "FA163E6728911EDAA5BFCEE21420A568",
				UUID: "fa163e67-2891-1eda-a5bf-cee21420a568",
				CreationDateTime: "20200528121755",
				SenderBusinessSystemID: "SOBASIC",
				RecipientBusinessSystemID: "0M2JP31"
			},
			Product @(plantListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , salesSpecificationListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , valuationAreaListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , descriptionListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , globalTradeItemNumberListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , quantityConversionListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , quantityCharacteristicListCompleteTransmissionIndicator: true , changeOrdinalNumberValue: "00000000000000000001" , reconciliationPeriodCounterValue: "1"): {
				ProductInternalID: attributes.queryParams.productId,
				UnformattedProductInternalID: "TEST444",
				ReceiverProductInternalID: attributes.queryParams.productId,
				UnformattedReceiverProductInternalID: "TEST444",
				ProductTypeCode: "MAT",
				IndustrySectorCode: "M",
				BaseMeasureUnitCode: "BG",
				BasicViewMaintainedIndicator: true,
				Description @(actionCode: "04"): {
					Description @(languageCode: "en"): "Material test 444"
				},
				QuantityConversion @(actionCode: "04"): {
					Quantity @(unitCode: "BG"): "1.0",
					CorrespondingQuantity @(unitCode: "BG"): "1.0"
				},
				StorageSpecification: {
					MinimumRemaininigShelfLifeCode: 0
				}
			}
		}
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<s4hana-soap:invoke doc:name="Invoke" config-ref="SAP_S4_HANA_SOAP_Config" service="https://my303109-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/bc/srt/wsdl_ext/flv_10002S111AD1/srvc_url/sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/productmdmbulkreplicaterequest" operation="ProductMDMBulkReplicateRequest_In" customQueryParams='#["MessageId" : uuid()]'/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message='#["Product successfully created"]'/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
payload.body]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector 2.6 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SAP S/4HANA SOAP provides full support for the SOAP APIs exposed by SAP
S/4HANA ERP and, starting with 2.1.x, provides support also for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise ERP.

The main class of an extension is the entry point from which configurations, connection providers,
operations, and sources are declared.

## Configurations

---

### Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_BasicAuthentication">Basic Authentication</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_CertificateAuthentication">Certificate Authentication</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2ClientCredentialsAuthentication">Oauth2 Client Credentials Authentication</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default character encoding to use in all the messages. If not specified, the default charset in the mule configuration will be used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for the request.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeout configurations.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WSA200508</p></li><li><p>WSA200408</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>WS-Addressing version. Defaults to WSA200508.</p></td><td><p>WSA200508</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference of the endpoint where the message originated from.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Basic Authentication

Basic authentication is the simplest way of enforcing access controls because it does not require
cookies, session identifiers, or login pages. Instead, it uses standard fields in the HTTP header,
removing the need for handshakes. This mechanism provides no confidentiality protection for the
transmitted credentials. They are merely encoded with Base64 in transit, but not encrypted or hashed
in any way.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Service base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL of the service on SAP S/4HANA to be accessed from the connector.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account username with which to log in to SAP S/4HANA.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account password with which to log in to SAP S/4HANA.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables automatic service discovery.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL to use for automatic services discovery.</p></td><td><p><code>/sap/bc/srt/wsil_ext</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Common HTTP directory for all SAP services.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unlisted services</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of services to add to the list of discovered services.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service discovery resolution timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for service discovery.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service Discovery Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeout configuration.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default HTTP headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default HTTP headers to include in the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default query parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default query parameters to include in the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol then the user needs to configure at least the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Certificate Authentication

An X.509 certificate is a digital certificate based on the widely accepted International
Telecommunications Union (ITU) X.509 standard, which defines the format of public key infrastructure
(PKI) certificates. They are used to manage identity and security in internet communications and
computer networking. X.509 authentication requires a discovery service configuration, a valid
certificate, and a secure connection (HTTPS).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Service base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL of the service on SAP S/4HANA to be accessed from the connector.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables automatic service discovery.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL to use for automatic services discovery.</p></td><td><p><code>/sap/bc/srt/wsil_ext</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Common HTTP directory for all SAP services.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unlisted services</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of services to add to the list of discovered services.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service discovery resolution timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for service discovery.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service Discovery Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeout configuration</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default HTTP headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default HTTP headers to include in the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default query parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default query parameters to include in the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol then the user needs to configure at least the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Authentication

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

OAuth 2.0 authentication provides a secure way of authenticating against the resource using the
provided client ID and client secret. Authentication is ensured using an access token generated by
the resource. The generated token has a limited lifespan and is automatically refreshed by
initiating a refresh dance, after the token expires.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Service base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL of the SAP S/4HANA SOAP service to access from the connector.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables automatic service discovery.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discovery Service Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL to use for automatic services discovery.</p></td><td><p><code>/sap/bc/srt/wsil_ext</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Common HTTP directory for all SAP services.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unlisted services</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of services that will be added to the list of discovered services, for example: <code>/sap/bc/srt/wsdl_ext/flv_10002S111AD1/srvc_url/sap/bc/srt/scs_ext/sap/creditmanagementaccountbyidqu1</code><br>If the <code>Service directory</code> parameter is not specified, then the HTTP directory needs to be prepended before every service name in this list, in the following format: <code>{directory}/{service-name}</code>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service discovery resolution timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for service discovery.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service Discovery Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeout configuration</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default HTTP headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default HTTP headers the message should include.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default query parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default query parameters the request should include.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol then the user needs to configure at least the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://{host-name}/{oauth-service}/{generate-token-resource}</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes requested during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to the object store used to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, the default object store is automatically provisioned by Mule.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Operations

- [Invoke](#Invoke)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

---

### Listener Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Default character encoding to use in all the messages. If not specified, the default charset in the Mule configuration is used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>HTTP Listener</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to the global HTTP Listener that will be used to receive SOAP S/4HANA messages.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>WSDL Paths</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#WsdlPath">WSDL Path</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the locations of the WSDL definitions.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier of this element; used by other components to reference it.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Security</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ListenerSecurityConfig">Listener Security Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Message-level security configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Sources

- [Outbound Message Listener](#OutboundMessageListener)

## Supported Operations

- [Invoke](#Invoke)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

## Invoke

`<s4hana-soap:invoke>`

Consumes an operation from an S/4HANA SOAP Web Service.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Web Service Addressing</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AddressingSettings">Addressing Settings</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configuration of the WS-Addressing specific SOAP headers.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Soap Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional soap headers to be wrapped in the soap envelope.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operation name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Operation name of the service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content of the message.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit to use in the timeout configurations</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom HTTP headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers that the request will include. The headers specified here will be merged with the default headers specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom query parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters that the request will include. The query parameters specified here will be merged with the default query parameters specified in the config.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p><a href="#SoapOutputEnvelope">SOAP Output Envelope</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SoapAttributes">SOAP Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- S4HANA-SOAP:BAD_REQUEST
- S4HANA-SOAP:CONNECTIVITY
- S4HANA-SOAP:PROCESSING_ERROR
- S4HANA-SOAP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Unauthorize

`<s4hana-soap:unauthorize>`

Deletes all of the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without repeating the authorization dance.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

## Sources

## Outbound Message Listener

`<s4hana-soap:outbound-message-listener>`

Creates an input source that listens for incoming SAP S/4HANA SOAP outbound messages.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path where server listens for the outbound messages.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allowed Methods</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of methods. Leave empty to allow all.</p></td><td><p>GET, POST</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message type the source listens for.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Allowed S/4HANA Systems</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#AllowedOutboundSystemParameter">Allowed Outbound System Parameter</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter for securing the Outbound Message Listener to receive outbound messages only from allowed S/4HANA systems.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should be executed only on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>On Capacity Overload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WAIT</p></li><li><p>FAIL</p></li><li><p>DROP</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Strategy that the Mule applies when the flow receives more messages to process than it has the capacity for</p></td><td><p>WAIT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason Phrase</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#OutboundMessageAttributes">Outbound Message Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Listener Config](#ListenerConfig)

## Types

### Proxy Configuration

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host where the proxy requests are sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port where the proxy requests are sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Non Proxy Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of hosts against which the proxy should not be used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ntlm Domain</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Domain to authenticate against the proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure
communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured
using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol then the user needs to configure at least
the keystore in the `tls:context` child element of the `listener-config`.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Truststore</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Location of the truststore, which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Algorithm used by the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Location of the keystore, which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the keystore contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Algorithm used by the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the truststore) if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance can be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Listener Security Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Service Credentials</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#UsernamePasswordSecurityStrategyConfig">Username Password Security Strategy Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Listener client credentials configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Signature Verification Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#TrustStoreStrategy">Trust Store Strategy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#DefaultStrategy">Default Strategy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Strategy used to verify signed SOAP messages.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Username Password Security Strategy Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Clients</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#UsernamePasswordEntry">Username Password Entry</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>List of authorized usernames and passwords.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Username Password Entry

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Clientâs username.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Clientâs password.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Default Strategy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CACerts Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password for the default Java cacerts truststore. This store is located in <code>&lt;JAVA_HOME&gt;/lib/security/cacerts</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>changeit</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store Strategy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Trust store path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to your truststore. This is either an absolute path or a path relative to your <code>resources</code> folder.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust store password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password for the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust store type</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JCEKS</p></li><li><p>JKS</p></li><li><p>PKCS12</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of truststore.</p></td><td><p><code>PKCS12</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### SOAP Output Envelope

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Attachments</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
